padded_coif.name: padded coif
padded_coif.lore: A close-fitting cap of quilted linen stuffed with sheep's wool, designed to cushion the skull without impeding movement. Favored by those who prefer swift thinking over heavy protection — it offers little defense but weighs almost nothing.

leather_cap.name: leather cap
leather_cap.lore: A close-cropped leather cap, hardened with oil and shaped on a wooden form. Popular among scouts and rogues, it keeps the hair back without clanking and provides a genuine measure of protection against glancing blows.

iron_helm.name: iron helm
iron_helm.lore: A simple iron pot helm with a fixed nasal guard and cheek plates. The design dates back centuries and has never been improved because it has never needed to be — it protects what matters and costs little enough to leave behind if you must run.

chain_coif.name: chain coif
chain_coif.lore: Thousands of hand-riveted rings form this close-fitting hood, which conforms perfectly to the skull and neck. The chain drape is uncomfortable in sustained wearing but provides excellent protection against slashing attacks to the head and throat.

steel_helm.name: steel helm
steel_helm.lore: A segmented steel spangenhelm with a riveted brow band and hinged cheek guards. The polished surface deflects glancing blows with impressive efficiency, and the thoughtful ventilation prevents the overheating that plagues lesser designs.

great_helm.name: great helm
great_helm.lore: The full-face steel great helm encloses the entire head in a shell of tempered metal, with only a narrow vision slit revealing the wearer's eyes. Pierce attacks find no purchase on its seamless exterior; the trade-off is severely restricted peripheral vision and hearing.

plate_helm.name: plate helm
plate_helm.lore: A master armourer's plate helm with a hinged visor and articulated neck guard. The curved surfaces are calculated to deflect sword blows in any direction, and the padding within is shaped memory-leather that conforms to a specific wearer's skull.

crystal_helm.name: crystal helm
crystal_helm.lore: Faceted from resonant spell-crystal over twelve months of careful work, this helm intercepts magical waves before they reach the mind within. Enchanters who observe it note that spells cast at the wearer seem to slide off its angled faces like water.

dragonscale_helm.name: dragonscale helm
dragonscale_helm.lore: Constructed from the head-plates of a fire wyrm, this helm carries a vestigial draconic resistance to flame — fire seems simply not to reach the face of whoever wears it. The scales shift between gold and amber as the light changes, beautiful and deadly in equal measure.

adamantine_helm.name: adamantine helm
adamantine_helm.lore: The adamantine full helm is among the most protective headgear ever devised. Blunt impacts that would cave ordinary plate simply transfer their force sideways along the impossibly dense metal, leaving the wearer with a ringing head but an intact skull.

cloth_armor.name: cloth armor
cloth_armor.lore: A thick-woven garment of sturdy cloth offering minimal protection but maximum freedom of movement. Scholars and mages favor it for exactly this reason — their safety comes from knowing what's coming, not from what they're wearing when it arrives.

padded_armor.name: padded armor
padded_armor.lore: Layers of linen stuffed with wool and stitched into a fitted body garment, this armor is the first step beyond bare skin. Soldiers in poorer armies wear nothing else, and it turns aside more attacks than one might expect simply through its bulk and give.

leather_armor.name: leather armor
leather_armor.lore: Vegetable-tanned leather hardened in sections and fitted to a standard body form. The supple material moves with the wearer rather than against them, making this the most popular choice among those who need protection without sacrificing agility.

ring_mail.name: ring mail
ring_mail.lore: Iron rings punched through a leather backing in overlapping rows, heavier than simple leather but significantly more protective. The distinctive clicking sound of ring mail has warned many a monster that a well-armed opponent approaches.

scale_mail.name: scale mail
scale_mail.lore: Overlapping steel scales riveted to a leather backing, mimicking the natural armor of reptiles. The clever overlapping design causes slashing weapons to glance sideways along the scales rather than finding purchase, much to the frustration of would-be assassins.

chain_mail.name: chain mail
chain_mail.lore: Thousands of individually riveted iron rings interlocked in a four-in-one pattern, this hauberk is the backbone of professional soldiers' equipment. Both slashing and piercing weapons struggle to find a gap in the continuously flexible surface.

banded_mail.name: banded mail
banded_mail.lore: Horizontal bands of articulated steel plate sewn over a mail foundation, providing considerably more rigid protection while retaining some flexibility. Elite infantry favor it as the best compromise between armor weight and combat endurance.

splint_mail.name: splint mail
splint_mail.lore: Vertical steel splints lashed tightly between layers of heavy leather create a rigid body protection that defies blunt impact far better than chain. Warriors who fight giants and trolls swear by it — the vertical arrangement spreads impact force efficiently across the whole garment.

half_plate.name: half plate
half_plate.lore: Shaped steel plate covering the torso, shoulders, and upper arms, worn over a chain shirt for gap coverage. Knights errant who cannot afford full plate often settle for this, finding it provides extraordinary protection where the body is most vulnerable.

full_plate.name: full plate
full_plate.lore: The pinnacle of conventional armorsmithing — every plate fitted specifically to a single wearer's body, every joint articulated for full combat mobility. A full suit represents months of a master smith's work and costs more than most adventurers earn in a year.

crystal_plate.name: crystal plate
crystal_plate.lore: Grown rather than forged, this armor was cultivated from a single crystal seed over decades in a magically controlled environment. The resulting plates are lighter than steel yet harder, and the lattice structure dissipates both fire and cold energy with remarkable efficiency.

dragonscale_armor.name: dragonscale armor
dragonscale_armor.lore: Harvested from a dragon of ancient lineage, each scale on this armor is broader than a human palm and harder than forged steel. The draconic essence still saturates the scales, granting the wearer dramatic resistance to fire and acid — the dragon's own weapons turned to defense. This is one of the three pieces of the Dragonslayer's Set. When worn alongside the Dragonbone Longsword and the Dragonslayer's Ring, the wearer gains immunity to fear and +2 to all attacks against dragons.

adamantine_armor.name: adamantine armor
adamantine_armor.lore: The heaviest wearable armor in existence, forged from the most dense metal known to mortalkind. Both slashing and blunt attacks reduce to a fraction of their normal effect against adamantine — the metal simply refuses to deform. Wearing it requires exceptional strength and constitution, but those who do approach invulnerability against physical assault.

leather_bracers.name: leather bracers
leather_bracers.lore: Simple oil-hardened leather strapped over the forearms, protecting the vulnerable tendons and veins from glancing cuts. Archers prize them additionally for the bowstring snap they prevent — a small consideration that saved many an arm over years of practice.

iron_bracers.name: iron bracers
iron_bracers.lore: Riveted iron plates encasing the forearm from wrist to elbow. Soldiers wear these when expecting to block weapon strikes with their arms — a maneuver discouraged but sometimes unavoidable in close quarters where shield positioning breaks down.

steel_bracers.name: steel bracers
steel_bracers.lore: Articulated steel plates covering the forearm and upper wrist, hinged at the elbow for mobility. The flared wrist guard is designed to catch and redirect blade strikes, turning parries into counterattack opportunities for a skilled fighter.

plate_bracers.name: plate bracers
plate_bracers.lore: Full-coverage plate vambraces from a master armourer's workshop, shaped to follow the arm's musculature precisely. The curved surfaces deflect slashing attacks with geometric precision, and the articulated elbow cowter allows full arm extension without binding.

adamantine_bracers.name: adamantine bracers
adamantine_bracers.lore: Adamantine vambraces so dense that blocking a maul strike with them is effectively the same as blocking with a wall. Fighters wearing these will sometimes deliberately absorb blows to the forearms that would have been problematic to parry, exploiting the metal's almost supernatural durability.

cloth_gloves.name: cloth gloves
cloth_gloves.lore: Knitted from tight-spun wool, these gloves offer almost no physical protection but keep the hands warm and improve grip in damp dungeon passages. Scholars wear them to protect sensitive fingertips during extended reading sessions underground.

leather_gloves.name: leather gloves
leather_gloves.lore: Soft calfskin gloves fitted to exact finger measurements, providing excellent tactile feel and modest protection for the hands. Thieves prize gloves of this quality because they permit lock manipulation without leaving fingerprints — a practical consideration in any profession involving entry without invitation.

chain_gauntlets.name: chain gauntlets
chain_gauntlets.lore: Chain link gauntlets with a leather palm lining for grip, covering the hands and extending partway up the forearm. The flexible construction preserves weapon-handling dexterity while protecting the fingers and knuckles from cut damage.

steel_gauntlets.name: steel gauntlets
steel_gauntlets.lore: Articulated steel gauntlets with segmented finger plates, allowing full grip while protecting the back of the hand completely. Experienced fighters note that the hardened knuckles make unarmed strikes considerably more painful — an unintended but appreciated bonus.

plate_gauntlets.name: plate gauntlets
plate_gauntlets.lore: Plate gauntlets with fully articulated finger protection and reinforced knuckle guards that distribute blunt impact across the entire hand structure. Blacksmiths who work near forges have been known to covet them simply as heat protection — though their martial applications are considerably more dramatic.

adamantine_gauntlets.name: adamantine gauntlets
adamantine_gauntlets.lore: Gauntlets of pure adamantine, each finger plate a masterwork of segmented engineering. The metal is so dense that a punch from these gauntlets strikes with the force of a hammer blow; wearing them requires adjusting one's grip on weapons, as the enhanced weight changes every swing's momentum.

leather_leggings.name: leather leggings
leather_leggings.lore: Boiled leather shaped into fitted leg protection, covering thigh to knee in a single articulated piece. Scouts favor these over heavier alternatives because they protect the legs without the weight penalty that turns a sprint into a jog.

chain_leggings.name: chain leggings
chain_leggings.lore: Chain mail chausses covering leg and thigh, traditionally paired with a full hauberk for complete coverage. The flexible construction allows a full range of kicks and strides while turning aside most cutting attacks to the lower body.

steel_greaves.name: steel greaves
steel_greaves.lore: Articulated steel greaves covering shin and knee with a hinged poleyn for joint mobility. Infantry commanders discovered that unprotected legs were the primary target of low combat strikes, and the adoption of these greaves dramatically reduced debilitating lower-body wounds.

plate_greaves.name: plate greaves
plate_greaves.lore: Complete plate protection from hip to ankle, with articulated cuisses, poleyns, and greaves working in mechanical harmony. The engineering required to make these move with a wearer without binding represents a century of accumulated armorsmithing refinement.

adamantine_greaves.name: adamantine greaves
adamantine_greaves.lore: Adamantine greaves with a polished-dark surface that seems to absorb light. The material's extreme hardness means slashing attacks aimed at the legs — a classic strategy against armored opponents — simply fail to penetrate, leaving the wearer free to ignore the lower-body strikes most warriors rely upon.

leather_boots.name: leather boots
leather_boots.lore: Hard-soled boots of decent leather, worn smooth at the heel from long travel. They protect the feet from dungeon debris and the constant moisture of stone passages, while the flexible uppers allow silent movement when circumstances demand it.

iron_boots.name: iron boots
iron_boots.lore: Iron sabatons with articulated toe plates and a riveted ankle guard. The heavy iron construction means underground creatures who try to attack the feet find the experience as productive as attacking a stone floor — though the weight makes running feel like wading through mud.

steel_boots.name: steel boots
steel_boots.lore: Articulated steel sabatons shaped to follow the foot's natural flex, with reinforced toe caps that have broken more than a few monster ribs when their wearers chose to kick rather than step back. The polished steel exterior is a point of professional pride for every armourer who produces them.

plate_boots.name: plate boots
plate_boots.lore: Full-coverage plate boots from ankle to toe, with a complex mechanism of overlapping plates that somehow allows normal walking. Worn with full plate, they complete a suit of protection that covers every inch of the body in solid metal.

adamantine_boots.name: adamantine boots
adamantine_boots.lore: Adamantine boots so dense that every step leaves faint impressions in stone floors. Floor-level attacks — the trademark of dungeon ambushers who attack from below — find no purchase whatsoever, and the sound of these boots approaching has been known to send intelligent monsters fleeing without combat.

cloth_cloak.name: cloth cloak
cloth_cloak.lore: A wide, rough-woven woolen cloak that offers little protection but significant warmth. In the cold stone passages of deep dungeons, many adventurers have decided that comfort matters more than combat statistics — these people often survive longer than their more heavily armored companions.

leather_cloak.name: leather cloak
leather_cloak.lore: Water-proofed leather, cut wide and hung to the heel, with an inner layer of dense wool. The material turns aside glancing blows that would have opened cuts on an unprotected back, and the dark color makes its wearer less visible in shadowed corridors.

ring_cloak.name: ring cloak
ring_cloak.lore: A cape fashioned from interlocking iron rings, heavier than it looks and surprisingly protective against attacks from behind. Cavalry officers pioneered this design to guard their back and flanks during mounted combat; it translated well to dungeon fighting where rearward threats are constant.

crystal_cloak.name: crystal cloak
crystal_cloak.lore: Woven from spell-crystal filaments as fine as spider silk, this cloak shimmers like running water in firelight. The crystal lattice is naturally hostile to directed magical energy — spells that strike it lose coherence, dissipating harmlessly before they can affect the wearer.

dragonscale_cloak.name: dragonscale cloak
dragonscale_cloak.lore: This sleek cloak is fashioned from the wing membrane and smaller scales of a shadow-drake, a draconic subspecies known for their natural stealth and venomous bite. The scales absorb fire and seem to drink in ambient light, making the wearer unusually difficult to spot in dim conditions. This is one of the three pieces of the Shadow Walker's Set. When worn alongside the Adamantine Dagger and the Shadow Walker's Ring, the wearer gains a poison strike on all attacks and moves silently regardless of armor worn.

cloth_shirt.name: cloth shirt
cloth_shirt.lore: A plain linen undershirt, worn beneath armor to prevent chafing and absorb sweat. Adventurers who skip this basic layer quickly develop the kind of skin problems that make donning heavy armor a daily ordeal — comfort matters more than newcomers suspect.

padded_shirt.name: padded shirt
padded_shirt.lore: A fitted gambeson of quilted canvas stuffed with raw wool, worn as both a standalone garment and as padding beneath heavier armor. The thick quilting distributes impact force across a wide area, making it surprisingly effective against blunt weapons.

chain_shirt.name: chain shirt
chain_shirt.lore: A fine mesh of riveted rings worn beneath outer armor to protect gaps and joints. The shirt's true value is revealed when a blade finds the seam in a breastplate — instead of drawing blood, it meets the chain and glances away, foiled by thousands of interlocking circles of steel.

mithril_shirt.name: mithril shirt
mithril_shirt.lore: Lighter than silk, stronger than iron — the legendary mithril shirt has been sought by every warrior who ever heard of it. The silvery rings are impervious to corrosion and naturally hostile to magical energy, deflecting enchantments that would otherwise pass through conventional armor unimpeded.

adamantine_shirt.name: adamantine shirt
adamantine_shirt.lore: Adamantine rings fine enough to pass as chain mail, yet possessing all the hardness of the parent metal. Even worn alone it provides extraordinary protection against cutting weapons — slashing attacks that would split lesser mail simply fail to sever the adamantine links, regardless of force applied.
